bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Skarmory] is the Champions MA hazard [lead] built around base 140 Defense and the ability to solo-stack both [spikes] and [stealth-rock] — a role almost nothing else in the format fills. Offense is irrelevant here; this is the installer, the resetter, and the physical [wall] rolled into one slot.

## Strengths
A physically defensive 252 HP / 252 Def [impish] spread turns base 140 Defense into numbers that eat most meta physical attacks at neutral or better. [Steel] typing resists [Bug], [Grass], [Dragon], and [Fighting] while being immune to [Poison] and (via [Flying]) [Ground], so the switch-in pool is enormous. [sturdy] guarantees one layer of [spikes] on turn 1 even into a [lead] that clicks a super-effective move, and [roost] brings the HP back for another cycle in the late game.

## Weaknesses & Answers
2x [Fire] and 2x [Electric] are the bill-killers. Heatran's [lava-plume] and Magnezone's [thunderbolt] simply outpace [roost] recovery. Base 70 Special Defense means any competent special attacker overwhelms the set, and a well-timed [taunt] shuts down the entire hazard plan before it starts. Magic-[bounce] leads [bounce] the hazards back, so avoid matchups where Mega Sableye or Espeon is the opposing [lead].

## Recommended Moveset
[roost] is mandatory to [recycle] HP across the entire game. [spikes] is the whole reason to run [Skarmory] — the most efficient multi-layer hazard in the game and the primary damage source for pivot-heavy teams to exploit. [iron-head] is the [Steel] STAB that keeps Fairies and [Ice] attackers from getting a free click on switch. [whirlwind] is the anti-setup tool: any [dragon]-dance or [calm-mind] sweeper that boosts in front of [Skarmory] gets phased out and the layers go to work on their backline. Swap [whirlwind] for [stealth-rock] on teams that need both entry hazards.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[leftovers] is the only real choice — the per-turn 6% heal stacks with [roost] for effectively infinite longevity. Ability is [sturdy], which guarantees the turn-1 [spikes] layer even into an opposing super-effective [lead]. Tera [Dragon] removes the [Fire] weakness and is the cleanest tournament pick, while Tera [Fighting] answers both [Electric] and [Rock] weaknesses in one swap for defensive flexibility.

## Teammates & Synergy
Dedicated [Fire] and [Electric] answers (Swampert, Tyranitar) are mandatory partners to cover [Skarmory]'s exact weaknesses. A heavy-hitting setup sweeper on the team maximizes the value of every hazard layer [Skarmory] puts down. [Ghost] partners deny opposing [defog] and preserve the layers through the late game. A mental-herb user or priority [taunt] teammate protects the hazard plan when [Skarmory] faces an anti-[lead].

## Play Patterns
Lead [Skarmory] on turn 1 and click [spikes] immediately. If hit with a super-effective move, [sturdy] eats it and the next click is another layer or [stealth-rock]. Save [roost] for sub-50% HP so no recovery is wasted. When the opponent brings in a setup sweeper, fire [whirlwind] immediately to reset their boosts into your hazards. Tera only when a [Fire] or [Electric] move is telegraphed — it is the difference between keeping your installer alive and losing the late game.
